back: A vector space V over a field F is a set equipped with two operations: vector addition and scalar multiplication, satisfying 10 axioms (closure, commutativity, associativity, identity, inverses, distributivity).

front: What are the 10 vector space axioms?
back: 1) Closure under addition 2) Commutativity 3) Associativity 4) Additive identity 5) Additive inverse 6) Closure under scalar multiplication 7) Scalar distributivity 8) Vector distributivity 9) Associativity of scalars 10) Scalar identity

A subset W of a vector space V is a subspace if W is non-empty, closed under vector addition, and closed under scalar multiplication (i.e., contains zero vector and is closed under linear combinations).
A vector v in V is a linear combination of vectors v1, v2, ..., vn if v = a1v1 + a2v2 + ... + anvn, where a1, a2, ..., an are scalars from the field F.

front: Define the Span of a set of vectors
back: The span of a set S = {v1, v2, ..., vn} is the set of all linear combinations of vectors in S, denoted as span(S) or span{v1, v2, ..., vn}. It is the smallest subspace containing S.
Vectors v1, v2, ..., vn are linearly independent if a1v1 + a2v2 + ... + anvn = 0 implies all scalars a1, a2, ..., an = 0. Otherwise, they are linearly dependent.

front: Define a Basis of a Vector Space
back: A basis is a set of linearly independent vectors that span the entire vector space V. Every vector in V can be uniquely expressed as a linear combination of basis vectors.

front: What is the Dimension of a Vector Space?
back: The dimension is the number of vectors in any basis of the vector space. If a basis has n vectors, the space is n-dimensional, denoted as dim(V) = n.

front: Is R^2 a subspace of R^3?
back: No, R^2 is not a subspace of R^3 because R^2 vectors have only 2 components while R^3 vectors have 3. However, any plane through the origin in R^3 is a 2-dimensional subspace.
Every vector space has at least two subspaces: the set containing only the zero vector {0} (called trivial or zero subspace) and the vector space V itself (called improper subspace).

front: How do you check if a set is a subspace?
back: To verify W is a subspace: 1) Check if W is non-empty (contains zero vector) 2) Check closure under addition: if u, v in W, then u+v in W 3) Check closure under scalar multiplication: if v in W and c is scalar, then cv in W.

front: What is a Linear Span in R^n?
back: The span of vectors in R^n is the set of all possible linear combinations. If you have 2 linearly independent vectors in R^3, their span is a plane through the origin. If you have 3 linearly independent vectors, their span is all of R^3.
A zero vector space (or trivial vector space) contains only one element, the zero vector. It has dimension 0 and serves as a subspace of every vector space.

front: What is meant by 'Closed under addition'?
back: A set S is closed under addition if for any two vectors u, v in S, their sum u+v is also in S. This is one of the essential properties required for a subspace.
